parsed_review.summary: CP00-164 is descriptor/contract-only. No real-data load, runtime permission evaluation, audit append, API handler execution, network I/O, UI render/DOM mutation, or AI-retrieval/analytics execution exists in the code; freezeSyntheticFixtureSetResult and MASTER_DATA_CP164_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION stamp every result and attestation flag false, and validators/tests assert them. Golden (2), failure (4: denied/cross-tenant/missing-context/security-trimming), review-required, audit-hint, and AI-analytics cases are all descriptor-only with customer-safe error codes; cross_tenant_case is a blocked-scenario descriptor (no cross-tenant data), security_trimming_case sets exposes_internal_claims/exposes_hidden_values=false, and each case carries no_real_data_check plus fixture_manifest.generated_from_real_client_data=false. Hidden source fields and raw permission/audit/document bodies are enumerated in leak_guard.prohibited_outputs and absent from descriptors. Contract is fully synced (schema v0.8->v0.9; current_pack CP00-164, Risk C, 150 units, range RP04.P04.M06.S06-RP04.P05.M06.S03; upstream CP00-163; next CP00-165/RP04.P05.M06.S04), and validateMasterDataCp164SyntheticFixtureSet contract-projection checks plus the .mjs script enforce no plan/contract drift. Claude gate pinned to claude-opus-4-8 / max / read_only. HRX remains embedded and LDIP not implemented (splits_hrx_product=false, implements_ldip=false). Pack is genuinely risk-sized (150 units across 2 phases / 12 micro-phases), not an arbitrary one-unit closeout. Descriptor-only and production_ready boundaries are adequately proven by the validators and tests. No P0/P1/P2 blockers identified.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-164 builds synthetic fixture set descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Claude diff-only context could not inspect the generated plan/manifest snapshot
findings.evidence: Coverage of the 150 units is enforced only via validateMasterDataCp164Coverage against the external closeout plan / cp00-164 manifest, neither of which is included in this diff. The deliverable distribution (claude_review 12, ui 29, security_audit 12, implementation 36, fixture 43, test 12, hermes_evidence 6) and phase split (RP04.P04 67 / RP04.P05 83) are internally consistent and sum to 150, and both the test and .mjs script fail-closed if the plan/manifest is missing, but the underlying plan units cannot be independently inspected from the diff alone.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: CP00-164 manifest includes the full plan_binding_snapshot and all 150 included units; closeout-pack validation checks the manifest against closeout-pack-plan.json.

findings.title: Leak guard is a synthetic substring tripwire rather than a semantic dynamic-data validator
findings.evidence: The leak guard (prohibited_output_absent / all_customer_facing_descriptors_trimmed) is a substring tripwire over prohibited token names on the serialized descriptor, not a semantic data check. Adequate here because every descriptor value is a hardcoded synthetic string or fixture-ID reference, but it would not detect a leak of sensitive values that do not match the token names if descriptors are later templatized with dynamic content. The request-param overrides (fixture_case_id/safe_error_code) are synthetic affordances and are still caught by this guard.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: CP00-164 is synthetic-only and uses hardcoded descriptor values; future real-data/rendering packs must replace substring checks with explicit value allow-lists.
